name: inchworm
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet hip-width apart.
Bend forward at the waist and place your hands on the ground in front of you.
Walk your hands forward until you are in a high plank position, with your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Pause for a moment, then walk your hands back towards your feet, keeping your legs as straight as possible.
Once your hands reach your feet, stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Start in a standing position with your feet hip-width apart. Bend forward at the waist and place your hands on the ground in front of you. Walk your hands forward until you are in a high plank position, with your body in a straight line from head to toe. Pause for a moment, then walk your hands back towards your feet, keeping your legs as straight as possible. Once your hands reach your feet, stand back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
